previewText: Fresh Gwinnett market numbers, a five-minute drainage check, and a Saturday farmers market.

paragraphs: We are right in the middle of a Georgia summer, when a sunny afternoon can turn into a heavy downpour before dinner.
This week I have one useful market number, a quick check that can help protect your home after a storm, and a local Saturday outing.
Nothing complicated — just a few things worth knowing and sharing.

market.title: Gwinnett buyers have more choices
paragraphs: Realtor.com reported 3,408 active listings in Gwinnett County during May 2026. That was about 11.2% more than May of last year.
More inventory gives buyers a little more room to compare condition, location, and price instead of feeling pressure to jump at the first home they see.
For sellers, the message is simple: competition matters. A clean, well-prepared home with a sensible price is more likely to earn attention.
market.verificationNote: Source: Realtor.com Economic Research county inventory data for May 2026, updated June 3, 2026.

homeownerTip.title: Watch where the rainwater goes.
homeownerTip.intro: After the next good rain, take five minutes to walk around the outside of your home:
items: Do downspouts carry water several feet away from the foundation?
Is water collecting beside the house or across a walkway?
Are leaves or debris blocking a drain?
homeownerTip.closing: A small drainage adjustment can sometimes prevent a much larger moisture problem. If water keeps collecting near the home, ask a qualified professional to evaluate it.

localEvent.title: Suwanee Farmers Market
localEvent.intro: If you are looking for something local this weekend, here is one Gwinnett option worth checking out.
localEvent.copy: The Suwanee Farmers Market brings local growers and makers together at Town Center. It is an easy Saturday-morning stop for seasonal produce, locally made products, and a walk around the park.
localEvent.tip: Tip: Check the City of Suwanee’s official events page for the current date, hours, vendor information, and weather updates before heading out.

cartoon.imageAlt: A couple watching a downspout pour rainwater into a tiny flowerpot
cartoon.caption: “Technically, the water is going somewhere.”
cartoon.closing: The foundation may have preferred a slightly better plan.
cartoon.imagePrompt: A family-friendly cartoon of a summer downspout draining into a comically tiny flowerpot, with no text in the artwork.
